question.text: Spot vs futures arbitrage yields a riskless profit when:

answer.text: The estimated fair futures price differs from the actual traded futures price
Spot and futures prices are identical
The commodity has high convenience yield
The market is in perfect contango

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Arbitrage opportunity exists when the estimated fair price (spot + carry) differs from the actual traded price. Buy the cheaper, sell the dearer. **The Trap:** Identical prices mean no arbitrage. High convenience yield affects pricing but doesn't guarantee arbitrage. Perfect contango may actually be at fair value. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers futures mispricing arbitrage. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Arbitrage windows close quickly as other traders exploit them — automated systems can detect and execute before manual traders can react.
